message.content: Adversaries exploit the Windows Installer utility, Msiexec, to execute malicious payloads, leveraging its native presence and digital signature by Microsoft to bypass security controls. Msiexec can run MSI packages and DLLs, and if the AlwaysInstallElevated policy is enabled, it can execute with SYSTEM privileges. This technique is used by various threat actors, including AppleJeus, Chaes, Clop, Duqu, and others, to install malware, disable security tools, and execute payloads. Common methods include using MSI files to start infection chains, injecting into Msiexec processes, and downloading malicious files over HTTP. Mitigation strategies include disabling the AlwaysInstallElevated policy, monitoring and restricting Msiexec usage through application control solutions, and employing behavioral analysis to detect abnormal Msiexec activities.
